M.title: HEMORRHAGE CONTROL
lines: > ASSESS for Unrecognized Hemorrhage and CONTROL Life-Threatening BLEEDING
> EXPOSE Wound to Assess TQ Necessity
> APPLY CoTCCC-recommended LIMB TQ if Indicated
> USE HEMOSTATIC DRESSING for COMPRESSIBLE Hemorrhage not Amenable to TQ
> Apply JUNCTIONAL TQ for GROIN // AXILLA Bleeding
> EXPOSE WOUND - Determine if TQ Still Needed
> If Bleeding NOT Controlled : Tighten TQ or ADD Second SIDE-BY-SIDE
> MARK TIME of Application on TQ and Casualty Card
> Do NOT remove if TQ in place >6 hours.

A.title: AIRWAY MANAGEMENT
lines: > CONSCIOUS patient   : talking normally = airway intact
> UNCONSCIOUS patient : tongue is most common obstruction
> Chin Lift or Jaw Thrust Maneuver
> Allow CONSCIOUS Casualty to Assume Position that Protects Airway
> NPA ( Contraindicated in Suspected Basilar Skull Fracture )
> Place UNCONSCIOUS Casualty in Recovery Position
NPA Sizing ( TIP of NOSE ... EAR LOBE , 7 mm // 8 mm , LUBRICATE )

R.title: RESPIRATION
lines: > Difficulty // Worsening Breathing AFTER Chest // Abdominal INJURY
> DECREASED Breath SOUNDS on AFFECTED SIDE
> JVD - Jugular Vein Distention ( Late Sign )
> APPLY Vented CHEST SEAL ( HyFin , SAM , Bolin )
> IF NO VENTED SEAL : APPLY Non-Vented and MONITOR Closely
> IF BREATHING WORSENS : Burp Seal or Perform NCD

C.title: CIRCULATION
lines: > If IV NOT OBTAINABLE : USE IO ( Humerus Preferred )
> Do NOT DELAY EVACUATION for IV Access Unless Lifesaving
> 1-2g IV // IO Flush ASAP // DO NOT GIVE > 3 Hours Post Injury
> CRITERIA : Hemorrhagic Shock , Amputations , Penetrating Torso ,

H.title: HYPOTHERMIA PREVENTION
lines: THE LETHAL TRIAD : Hypothermia + Acidosis + Coagulopathy = Death
> KEEP PROTECTIVE EQUIPMENT ON if feasible
> Get Casualty onto INSULATED SURFACE ASAP
> Use HPMK ( Hypothermia Prevention Management Kit )
> IF UNAVAILABLE : Dry Blankets , Poncho Liner , Sleeping Bag
> ELEVATE head 30° if NO SPINAL CONCERN

P.title: P - Pain Management
lines: > Mild-Moderate   ( ABLE to fight ) : Tylenol 650mg x2 + Meloxicam 15mg
> Moderate-Severe ( NOT in shock )  : OTFC 800mcg transmucosal
> Moderate-Severe ( IN shock )      : Ketamine 50mg IM/IN or 20mg slow IV/IO
A - Antibiotics : Moxifloxacin 400mg daily // Ertapenem 1g IV/IM daily
W - Wounds ( ANTIBIOTICS Recommended for ALL OPEN Combat Wounds )
> Inspect and Dress ALL known Wounds
> Full body sweep for ADDITIONAL Wounds
> Check Pulses Before AND After Splinting
> Immobilize Joint Above AND Below Fracture

B.title: BURNS
lines: > FACIAL Burns : Aggressively MONITOR AIRWAY ( EDEMA RISK )
Posterior Torso    : 18% (9% upper back, 9% lower back)
> Cover with DRY , STERILE Dressings ( BURN SHEET if Avalible )
> IF > 20% TBSA : IV fluids per Rule of Ten
> 3rd ( Full )         : White/Brown/Black , Dry , May NOT Hurt

F.title: CPR CONSIDERATIONS // PROCEDURE
lines: > Casualty does NOT have Obviously Fatal Wounds
CPR Should NOT Be Attempted If ...
> Denying Lifesaving Treatment to Other Casualties
> Center of Chest ( BETWEEN NIPPLES ) , Fingers Interlaced
> Keep Arms Straight , Shoulders Over Hands
> Push HARD - at Least 2 Inches Deep
> Push FAST - 100-120 Compressions per Minute ( Beat of "Stayin' Alive" )

I.title: IMIST HANDOVER REPORT
lines: T - Treatments Given ( TQ Time , Meds , etc. ) and Triage ( 1 ... 4 )
> India  : 1 x UK MIL , ZAP No SM1234
> Mike   : GSW to Right Thigh , 1423 hrs
> India  : Through-and-Through Right Thigh , No Bone Involvement
> Tango  : TQ Right Thigh 1425 , Wound Packed w/ Combat Gauze , 1g TXA IV
IMMEDIATE (T1) - RED    > Immediate Intervention // Will Die Without Treatment
DELAYED   (T2) - YELLOW > Needs Treatment but CAN Wait Without Life Threat
EXPECTANT (T4) - BLACK  > Injuries Incompatible with Survival Given Resources

9.title: Movement Methods // Actions on Extraction
lines: > Fastest   : Dragging Along the Long Axis of Patient's Body ( 2 Rescuers )
> Communicate Patient Status to TACEVAC Personnel
> Identify Receiving Care Provider on Evac Platform
> Establish DIRECT Contact ( Radio / Eye / Hand )
> Provide SIT Status ( Most Serious First ) :
